calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER GEORGE WOLFF's Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award.

Petitioner George Wolff's petition to vacate contractual arbitration award is denied.

The "Arbitration of All Disputes" exhibit to the parties' Litigation Retainer Agreement may allow expanded judicial review of the merits of the arbitrator's decision. (See Cable Connection, Inc. v. DirecTV, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 1334; but see Oaktree Capital Management, LP v. Bernard (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 60, 70-71.) Even assuming it does, however, Petitioner has failed to establish that the arbitrator committed legal error or exceeded his powers in rendering the award. The arbitrator expressly found that the retainer agreement extended only to the parties' initial consultation. The award on its face (at pg. 2) contradicts Petitioner's contention that the arbitrator "refused to look at other terms of the Litigation Retainer Agreement and construe them as a whole." Even if the applicable standard of review allowed Petitioner to challenge the arbitrator's factual finding, Petitioner has not provided the Court with the complete record of the arbitration, and therefore fails to show that the finding is unsupported by substantial evidence. (See Harshad & Nasir Corp. v. Global Sign Systems, Inc. (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 523, 537 [substantial evidence review "requires that we review the entire record, not merely the 'isolated bits of evidence selected by the [party challenging the award]' . . . . 'The ultimate test is whether it is reasonable for a trier of fact to make the ruling in question in light of the whole record.'"].)

Petitioner's contentions that "no . . . . limitations periods exist at all" as to claims brought in arbitration (Mot. at 3) and that the arbitrator lacked jurisdiction to decide the limitations issue (id. at 8) are inconsistent with Petitioner's position at the arbitration. (See id. at 5 [referring to Petitioner's "argument in his Closing Brief that Code [sic] Civil Proc. 337 provides the only Statute of Limitations applicable [sic] a dispute as to . . . the Written Litigation Retainer Agreement."].) It is also wrong as a matter of California law. Wagner Construction Co. v. Pacific Mechanical Corp. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 19, upon which Petitioner relies, stands for the opposite proposition: "Where, as here, the parties have agreed to arbitrate any dispute arising out of their contract, the affirmative defense that the statute of limitations has run is for the arbitrator rather than the court to decide." (Id. at 23 [emphasis added].)

The Court notes that both parties have incorrectly filed papers in this matter under the case number pertaining to the original petition to compel arbitration (CPF-16-515366), rather than under the case number pertaining to the instant petition to vacate the arbitration award (CPF-19-516575). To cure this mistake and avoid future confusion, the Court hereby consolidates the two actions for all purposes. Any future filings should be made under the original case number.

Petitioner objects to Respondent's proposed order submitted by email. Respondent to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners Memorandum Of Authorities On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Petitioner George Wolff's petition to compel arbitration is DENIED without prejudice to being refiled. The petition has been noticed for hearing with insufficient notice to respondents.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
